meta.one_liner: Chronic type-2 (IL-4/IL-13-driven), intensely itchy inflammatory skin disease — a maturing biologics + oral-JAK market anchored by Dupixent, now competing on itch, convenience, safety and durability.
meta.sales_note: Dupixent sales are all-indication franchise totals (AD is the majority). Market and segment figures are bottom-up [Estimated] and cross-checked against published envelopes.
meta.coverage_note: Demonstration atlas: structural data (assets, mechanisms, companies, phases) from live Open Targets + ClinicalTrials.gov + openFDA pulls; market/forecast figures are labeled [Estimated] or anchored to public company revenue. A full research pass would web-cite each market datum.
meta.signal_caption: Epithelial alarmins activate ILC2/Th2 cells → IL-4/IL-13 signal via IL-4Rα and JAK1/STAT6; IL-31 drives itch and IL-13 drives keratinocyte barrier dysfunction. Click any node for the science.

epidemiology.headline: ~223M globally · ~10% of children, ~7% of US adults
epidemiology.comorbidities: Type-2 comorbidities — asthma, allergic rhinitis, food allergy, eosinophilic esophagitis (the 'atopic march'); plus sleep disruption, anxiety/depression.
epidemiology.unmet: Pruritus control, durable remission, paediatric options, and steroid-phobia-driven under-treatment remain key gaps despite a crowded biologic/JAK field.
epidemiology.dlqi: AD carries one of the highest quality-of-life burdens in dermatology — itch and sleep loss dominate; POEM/DLQI improvements are core endpoints.
epidemiology.drug_survival: IL-4Rα/IL-13 biologics show high real-world persistence; oral JAKs face safety-driven discontinuation.
epidemiology.mortality: Not directly fatal; burden is chronic morbidity, itch and quality-of-life loss.

sites.note: Sensitive-site tolerability drives non-steroidal choice; ocular surface disease with IL-4Rα.
Chronic hand eczema is a distinct, disabling phenotype (delgocitinib approved EU).
Classic distribution; chronic lichenification with scratching.
Large label-expansion battleground; dupilumab down to 6 months.

lines.label: Emollients & barrier care
lines.note: Foundation of all AD care; barrier repair reduces flares.

lines.label: Topical anti-inflammatories
lines.agents: TCS · calcineurin inhibitors · crisaborole · ruxolitinib · roflumilast · tapinarof
lines.note: First pharmacologic line for mild-moderate; non-steroidals extend to face/folds.

lines.label: Oral JAK inhibitors
lines.note: Moderate-to-severe; fast and deep but second-line on class safety.

standard_of_care.note: Care escalates from barrier repair → topicals → phototherapy → biologics / oral JAKs. Basis: AAD & EADV guidelines.

moa_landscape.safety: Conjunctivitis / ocular surface disease; injection-site reactions; generally clean systemic profile — no boxed warning.
Conjunctivitis (lower than IL-4Rα for some); injection-site reactions.
Well tolerated; targets itch; peripheral effects under study.
JAK-class boxed warning: serious infection, MACE, thrombosis, malignancy, mortality — orals are second-line after biologics in many labels.
Local; ruxolitinib carries the JAK class boxed warning by label despite topical use.
Emerging class; pyrexia/chills seen with OX40 agents; long dosing intervals a potential differentiator.
Local, steroid-sparing, well tolerated for chronic/sensitive-site use.
Calcineurin inhibitors carry a (theoretical) boxed malignancy warning; burning on application.

assets.name: dupilumab
assets.note: FY2024 Dupixent global net sales ~$14.15B (all indications); the category anchor

assets.name: tralokinumab
assets.note: first selective IL-13 mAb in AD

assets.name: nemolizumab
assets.note: targets pruritus / prurigo nodularis; approved AD 2024

assets.name: upadacitinib
assets.note: highest EASI-75 among orals; boxed warning (JAK class)

assets.name: baricitinib
assets.note: approved AD in EU/Japan; not US for AD; boxed warning

assets.name: amlitelimab
assets.note: non-depleting OX40L; Ph2 STREAM-AD positive; Ph3 ongoing

nodes.label: TSLP / IL-33 / IL-25
nodes.description: Epithelial alarmins released by a damaged skin barrier; they activate dendritic cells and ILC2s to initiate the type-2 response — the upstream trigger of AD.

nodes.label: ILC2 / Th2 cell
nodes.description: Type-2 innate lymphoid and T-helper cells that, once activated by alarmins and OX40 co-stimulation, secrete the effector cytokines IL-4, IL-13 and IL-31.

nodes.label: IL-4
nodes.description: Type-2 cytokine driving Th2 differentiation and IgE class-switch; signals via IL-4Rα — the target of dupilumab.

nodes.label: IL-13
nodes.description: The dominant effector cytokine in lesional AD skin; drives keratinocyte dysfunction, barrier defects and inflammation. Blocked by tralokinumab/lebrikizumab and (with IL-4) by dupilumab.

nodes.label: IL-31
nodes.description: The 'itch cytokine' — acts on sensory neurons via IL-31RA to drive pruritus, the defining symptom of AD. Blocked by nemolizumab.

nodes.label: Keratinocyte / skin barrier
nodes.description: Under type-2 cytokines the keratinocyte downregulates filaggrin and barrier proteins, letting allergens/microbes in and amplifying inflammation — the self-perpetuating barrier–immune loop.

nodes.label: Eczematous lesion + itch
nodes.description: The clinical outcome: itchy, inflamed, barrier-defective eczematous skin.

nodes.label: IL-4Rα
nodes.description: Shared receptor subunit for IL-4 and IL-13 signalling; blocking it (dupilumab) neutralizes both cytokines.

nodes.label: JAK1
nodes.description: Janus kinase transducing IL-4/IL-13/IL-31 receptor signalling to STAT6/STAT3; oral JAK1 inhibitors block the whole type-2 signalling node intracellularly.

nodes.label: STAT6
nodes.description: Transcription factor activated by JAK1 downstream of IL-4Rα; drives the type-2 gene programme.

nodes.label: OX40 / OX40L
nodes.description: Co-stimulatory axis sustaining pathogenic memory T cells; targeted by rocatinlimab (OX40) and amlitelimab (OX40L) for potential durable, low-frequency dosing.

strategy_map.d: AD is driven by the IL-4/IL-13 type-2 axis; the dominant goal is durable suppression of that inflammation.

ch.d: Biologics neutralising the core effector cytokines — the durable, best-tolerated backbone.
Neutralise both type-2 effectors at the shared receptor.
Blocks IL-4 and IL-13 signalling; the category anchor.
Selective IL-13 mAbs; q4w maintenance a convenience edge.
Intracellular JAK inhibition — fast, oral, deep, but second-line on class safety.
Block the intracellular node transducing IL-4/13/31.
Highest short-term EASI-75; JAK-class boxed warning.

strategy_map.d: Pruritus is the defining, quality-of-life-dominating symptom; dedicated itch mechanisms are a distinct battleground.

ch.d: Neuroimmune IL-31 signalling drives pruritus directly.
Rapid antipruritic effect via the IL-31 receptor.
Localised, steroid-sparing control for mild-moderate and maintenance.
Rapid local control; ruxolitinib carries a class boxed warning.
Non-steroidal agents extending to sensitive sites and children.

strategy_map.d: Target the alarmin/OX40 apex for disease modification and low-frequency, potentially remission-inducing therapy.

ch.d: Hit the initiating signals rather than single effectors.
Non-depleting or depleting T-cell co-stimulation blockade; infrequent dosing bet.
Alarmin blockade; AD signal weaker than in asthma.

response_kinetics.note: Class-representative EASI-75 trajectories (label/NMA-informed; illustrative). Oral JAKs act fastest; IL-4/13 biologics climb steadily to durable control.

landmark_trials: EASI-75 ~44–51% vs ~12% placebo — established IL-4Rα blockade in AD.
EASI-75 ~70% — highest among orals; opened the oral-JAK tier.
EASI-75 ~43% with q4w maintenance — selective IL-13.
Rapid itch reduction via IL-31RA — validated the itch axis.
Durable responses off-drug with anti-OX40L — the remission bet.

trials.name: JADE COMPARE
endpoints: IGA 0/1 and EASI-75 at Week 12 vs placebo
Itch response (PP-NRS4) at Week 2 vs dupilumab
trials.readout: Oral abrocitinib 200mg showed faster itch response than dupilumab; a landmark oral-vs-biologic head-to-head in AD.
trials.inclusion: Adults with moderate-to-severe AD on background topical therapy.
trials.significance: First head-to-head of an oral JAK against the IL-4Rα biologic standard.

trials_focus.note: Cards carry the real NCT id, which deep-links to the exact ClinicalTrials.gov record. A full run pulls one card per pivotal programme by NCT id.

catalysts.significance: Second selective IL-13 biologic; q4w maintenance differentiator.
Non-steroidal topical AhR agonist extends into AD ≥2y.
First test of upstream co-stimulation blockade for durable, infrequent dosing.
Franchise defence via new indications (COPD, others) and younger AD ages.

deals.note: Sanofi acquired Kymab for the anti-OX40L mAb amlitelimab (KY1005).
Amgen/Kyowa Kirin co-develop the anti-OX40 mAb rocatinlimab (KHK4083).
Lilly acquired Dermira, gaining lebrikizumab (ex-US to Almirall).

segments.label: Oral JAK inhibitors
segments.note: Fast growth capped by class-safety positioning. [Estimated]

segments.label: Topical Rx (JAK/PDE4/AhR/CNI)
segments.note: Non-steroidal topicals take share from generic steroids. [Estimated]

segments.label: IL-31 / OX40 & emerging
segments.note: Nemolizumab + OX40 class if Ph3 delivers. [Estimated]

forecast.note: [Estimated] bottom-up from indication-attributed biologic revenue (Dupixent ~$14B FY2024 across indications, AD the majority) + emerging classes; cross-checked against published $15–20B (2024) AD envelopes.

scenarios.name: Base
scenarios.swing: IL-13 and topical non-steroidals expand the treated pool; OX40 class delivers a durable-dosing option; JAK stays second-line.

scenarios.name: Conservative
scenarios.swing: OX40 Ph3 disappoints; biosimilar/pricing pressure on the biologic backbone; JAK safety caps orals.

scenarios.name: Aggressive
scenarios.swing: Upstream/remission-inducing therapy reframes the category; strong paediatric expansion; net price defended on durability.

funnel.note: The core opportunity — steroid-phobia, access and under-referral, not efficacy.

competitive.posture: Defend and expand the type-2 backbone across indications + amlitelimab optionality
competitive.strength: Scale, prescriber entrenchment, cleanest biologic profile, paediatric breadth
competitive.vulnerability: Single-asset concentration; oral and IL-13 substitution at the margins

competitive.posture: Win on q4w convenience + IL-13 selectivity
competitive.vulnerability: Second into IL-13 behind tralokinumab; behind Dupixent on breadth

competitive.posture: Own the fast, deep oral segment
competitive.vulnerability: JAK-class boxed warning caps first-line use
Smaller commercial scale vs pharma majors
Late to the effector classes; OX40 efficacy/tolerability unproven
Narrower efficacy on inflammation vs IL-4/13

thesis.heading: State of the field
thesis.text: Atopic dermatitis has become the second great type-2 immunology market after asthma: a dominant IL-4Rα biologic (Dupixent) anchors a rapidly crowding field of selective IL-13 antibodies, oral JAK inhibitors, non-steroidal topicals and a new IL-31 itch mechanism, with upstream OX40/alarmin bets aiming at durable remission. The competitive question is shifting from 'can we clear the skin' to 'convenience, itch, safety and durability at what net price'.

glossary.def: Eczema Area and Severity Index — the primary AD efficacy score; EASI-75/90 = ≥75/90% improvement.
Investigator Global Assessment (0–4); IGA 0/1 = clear/almost-clear, a co-primary endpoint.
Patient-Oriented Eczema Measure — patient-reported symptom burden.
The IL-4/IL-13/IL-5 immune programme underlying AD, asthma and other atopic diseases.
Shared receptor subunit for IL-4 and IL-13; blocking it (dupilumab) neutralises both.
The dominant effector cytokine in lesional AD skin.
The 'itch cytokine' acting on sensory neurons; target of nemolizumab.
Kinase transducing type-2 receptor signalling; oral JAK1 inhibitors block it intracellularly.
Epithelial alarmin that initiates type-2 responses from a damaged barrier.
T-cell co-stimulatory axis targeted for durable, low-frequency therapy.
Progression from AD in infancy to food allergy, asthma and allergic rhinitis.
Barrier protein; loss-of-function is a key genetic risk factor for AD.
